# Block catalog

<!-- GENERATED by `npm run blocks` from src/blocks/ — do not edit by hand. -->

**27 blocks.** This file is the authoritative inventory: the skill reads it to choose
blocks, so it can never drift from what is actually implemented. Compose a video by picking one
**object** (typography or UI), a **transition** for each handoff, and — per the VARY laws — at
least two distinct text animations and a different primary handoff than the previous video.

Transitions are tagged `intro` (enter half), `outro` (exit half) or `full-tro` (a matched pair).
Pair an outro with an intro on the **same axis**: `push-off-left`/`glide-in` are X,
`scale-up-cut`/`scale-pop-in` are Z.

## Transition — 6 blocks

_scene-to-scene glue_

| block | tag | length | source | what it does |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `axis-handoff` | full-tro | 2.50s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @13.45s | X-axis pair. Outgoing object is thrown off the axis and cut mid-flight; incoming object decelerates onto the same axis. Objects shown are placeholders — drop in any text or UI block. |
| `depth-handoff` | full-tro | 2.17s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @3.9s | Z-axis pair. Outgoing object swells ~6% and is cut mid-swell; incoming object pops from 0.76 scale and decelerates into place. Objects shown are placeholders. |
| `push-off-left` | outro | 1.25s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @13.45s | Short throw, cut at peak. Accelerates the object just ~20px (1.5% of frame width) over 150ms and hard-cuts it while still fully on screen — measured −1 → −5 → −10 px/frame. Never animate it off-screen. |
| `scale-up-cut` | outro | 1.25s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @3.9s | Same law on Z. The object swells just ~6% over 100ms, accelerating (+5 → +16 → +24 px/frame), and is cut at peak while still fully visible. |
| `glide-in` | intro | 1.67s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @13.6s | Decelerates the object 80px onto the axis the outgoing one just left — long-tailed ease-out (~0.9s). Measured left edge 325 → 205, velocity −29 → −12 → −4 → −1 → 0. Pair with any text-animation block for per-word motion. |
| `scale-pop-in` | intro | 1.50s @60fps | C · GPT-5.5 @4.02s | Pops the object from 0.76 → 1.0 with a strong ease-out (~430ms, 62% of the change in the first 83ms), monotonic — no overshoot and no fade; scale alone carries the entrance. |

## Typography — 17 blocks

_pixel-point/animate-text_

| block | tag | length | source | what it does |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `soft-blur-in` | — | — | animate-text | Per-character fade-in with a gentle blur and upward motion. Apple's signature hero-title reveal. |
| `per-character-rise` | — | — | animate-text | Letters slide up from below with no blur — crisp, deliberate, kinetic. Apple's clean tvOS-style reveal. |
| `per-word-crossfade` | — | — | animate-text | Words gently fade into place one after another, with a short vertical drift for a calm keynote rhythm. |
| `spring-scale-in` | — | — | animate-text | Words pop in with a soft overshoot scale, like a physical spring settling into place. |
| `mask-reveal-up` | — | — | animate-text | Lines reveal upward with a soft masked feel and compact stagger. |
| `line-by-line-slide` | — | — | animate-text | Each line enters from the left with a staggered slide and exits to the right for a flowing paragraph reveal. |
| `typewriter` | — | — | animate-text | Per-character stepped reveal with a minimal editorial typing rhythm. |
| `micro-scale-fade` | — | — | animate-text | A calm, tiny scale pop used as subtle premium polish for labels and headings. |
| `shimmer-sweep` | — | — | animate-text | A subtle sweep across a clean headline, blending in while gliding from left to center. |
| `fade-through` | — | — | animate-text | A Material-style content transition: old fades out, new fades in with a soft delay. |
| `shared-axis-y` | — | — | animate-text | Per-word hard-cut transition with staircase timing for sharp editorial swaps. |
| `shared-axis-z` | — | — | animate-text | Scale-based shared-axis transition for focus shifts and context depth. |
| `blur-out-up` | — | — | animate-text | Words arrive clean and depart upward with increasing blur for airy exits. |
| `scale-down-fade` | — | — | animate-text | Subtle premium settle-in with a restrained scale-down fade on exit. |
| `focus-blur-resolve` | — | — | animate-text | A premium focus pull from heavy blur to crisp text, then a soft blur-out exit. |
| `bottom-up-letters` | — | — | animate-text | Letters rise from below in a pronounced staircase, one symbol at a time, with zero blur. |
| `top-down-letters` | — | — | animate-text | Letters descend from above in a pronounced staircase, one symbol at a time, with zero blur. |

## UI motion — 4 blocks

_GPT-5.5 house standard_

| block | tag | length | source | what it does |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `chip-tokenize` | — | — | C · GPT-5.5 | A typed @-mention converts in place into a colored tool chip ~0.1s after the word completes; typing continues around it. |
| `log-theater` | — | — | C · GPT-5.5 | Agent work as an accumulating checklist with app icons; the “Using X” header swaps as tools change; “Thinking” shimmers. |
| `log-theater-zoomed` | — | — | C · GPT-5.5 | The same log framed as a static macro crop — the window is bigger than the viewport, pinned to the top-left; the feed auto-scrolls inside. |
| `camera macro-push` | — | — | C · GPT-5.5 | The viewport pushes in ~1.6× over ~0.5s to showcase a hero component — strong ease-out that decelerates into the hold, no overshoot. |

`index.tsx`, so they are not usable blocks. Either register them or record why they are excluded
— a spec the skill can name but not render is worse than one that does not exist.
